mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals policy easing that lowers the discount rate on equities and supports risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the move is part of a sustained cutting cycle.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation re-accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains high, real policy becomes tighter and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 3.5% while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns more often than negative returns, or if the interaction term loses sign consistency across rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above the prior quarter's level signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not negative in most unemployment-upturn episodes, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC is consistently near zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is rising, job losses confirm a demand downturn and investors should price lower future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change falls below 0 and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-condition episodes do not show worse 20-day returns than either series alone, or if positive returns dominate after confirmed payroll-loss periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which lifts near-term earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-median 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign flips negative in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST from elevated levels indicates housing demand is weakening and the business cycle is rolling over, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing declines fail to precede negative 60-day returns, or if down-housing regimes do not outperform random benchmark forecasting.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL inflation is contained, the economy is expanding without forcing tighter pricing pressure, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-inflation growth condition does not beat the unconditional return distribution, or if returns turn negative when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO after prior weakness signals a manufacturing rebound that improves earnings breadth and cyclicals leadership, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if industrial production upturns do not precede above-median 60-day returns, or if the signal loses predictive power in post-recovery windows.
